heroCompanyDescription: Fisher & Paykel Healthcare Corporation Limited (ASX: FPH) is a New Zealand-headquartered global medical device company specialising in respiratory care, acute care, and the treatment of obstructive sleep apnea. The company is best known for its Optiflow high-flow nasal therapy system, which has become a standard of care in hospital intensive care and general ward settings globally, and its range of humidification systems used in surgical and respiratory applications.

hero.embedded_thesis: At A$30.00, the market appears to be pricing FPH as a quality compounder delivering on its upgraded FY26 guidance (NPAT NZD 450-470M) but with limited near-term re-rating potential. The forward P/E of 32.9x implies the market expects sustained mid-to-high single-digit earnings growth beyond FY26, consistent with continued hospital product adoption but not a step-change acceleration. The stock's proximity to its 52-week low of A$29.72 suggests investors are cautious about the Section 122 regulatory expiry in July 2026 and the sustainability of the currency tailwind that drove the February guidance upgrade. The current price does not appear to embed a meaningful probability of either a transformative M&A event or a significant guidance miss — it reflects a 'show me' stance pending FY26 full-year results and regulatory clarity.
hero.skew_description: The four hypotheses are weighted N2 (40%), N3 (28%), N1 (25%), and N4 (7%), reflecting a modestly cautious stance at initiation. The base case carries the highest weight given the verified guidance upgrade and FPH's track record, but the downside scenario is weighted nearly as heavily as the bull case due to the unresolved Section 122 risk and valuation sensitivity at 32.9x forward earnings.

position_in_range.note: World prices are indicative -- pending full W1-W4 valuation build

next_decision_point.metric: FY26 NPAT vs NZD 450-470M guidance range; FY27 revenue growth guidance; hospital product volume commentary
next_decision_point.thresholds: NPAT above NZD 470M and FY27 revenue growth guidance above 10% would support N1 re-rating toward A$36-40. NPAT below NZD 450M or FY27 guidance below 6% growth would validate N3 de-rating toward A$25-27. Section 122 commentary in the results presentation is a critical qualitative signal.

skew.rationale: The probability distribution is modestly skewed toward the base case (N2, 40%) with meaningful downside risk (N3, 28%) from the Section 122 regulatory overhang and valuation sensitivity. The bullish scenario (N1, 25%) is plausible given the guidance upgrade momentum, but the current price of A$30.00 — near the 52-week low — suggests the market has already partially priced in near-term uncertainty rather than the growth upside.

verdict.text: Initiating coverage on Fisher & Paykel Healthcare (ASX: FPH) with a NEUTRAL/HOLD stance at A$30.00. The market is pricing a quality compounder at a fair but demanding 32.9x forward P/E, with the February 2026 guidance upgrade (NPAT NZD 450-470M, revenue ~NZD 2.30B) already absorbed and the stock sitting just 0.9% above its 52-week low. We see the probability distribution as roughly balanced between the base case (40%) and a combination of bull (25%) and bear (28%) scenarios, with the Section 122 July 2026 expiry as the single most important near-term discriminator. The skew is modestly to the downside at current prices given the valuation sensitivity — a 10% de-rating in the forward multiple from 32.9x to ~29.5x would imply a stock price of approximately A$27, while a re-rating to 37x on a guidance beat would imply approximately A$34. We would become more constructive on a pullback toward A$27-28 or on evidence of a favourable Section 122 resolution.

featuredRationale: The four hypotheses are weighted N2 (40%), N3 (28%), N1 (25%), and N4 (7%), reflecting a modestly cautious stance at initiation. The base case carries the highest weight given the verified guidance upgrade and FPH's track record, but the downside scenario is weighted nearly as heavily as the bull case due to the unresolved Section 122 risk and valuation sensitivity at 32.9x forward earnings.

identity.overview: Fisher & Paykel Healthcare Corporation Limited (ASX: FPH) — auto-added to coverage. Full company overview pending analyst research.

hypotheses.title: N1: Growth/Recovery
hypotheses.description: FPH's hospital product portfolio sustains double-digit revenue growth beyond FY26, driven by expanding clinical adoption of high-flow nasal therapy (Optiflow) and new product cycles. Favourable currency tailwinds persist, NPAT exceeds the top of the NZD 450-470M guidance range, and the Section 122 regulatory uncertainty resolves benignly in July 2026, unlocking a valuation re-rating toward 40x forward earnings. This scenario requires continued hospital volume growth, stable or improving NZD/USD, and no material competitive disruption from Philips Respironics or ResMed.
supporting: FPH upgraded FY26 guidance to ~NZD 2.30B revenue and NPAT NZD 450-470M on 22 February 2026, demonstrating operational momentum
Hospital product portfolio cited as a key growth driver in the guidance upgrade, consistent with multi-year Optiflow adoption trends
Global healthcare spending grew 35% over five years (IHDS 2024), providing a supportive macro backdrop for premium medical device adoption
Favourable AUD/USD at US$0.70 provides earnings translation tailwind for NZD-reporting company listed on ASX
contradicting: Current trailing P/E of 49.2x already embeds significant growth expectations, leaving limited margin of safety
Pinnacle Investment Management reduced its holding below the 5% substantial threshold on 17 March 2026, a modest but notable institutional signal
Section 122 expiry in July 2026 introduces unquantified regulatory/reimbursement risk that could dampen hospital product demand

hypotheses.title: N2: Base Case
hypotheses.description: FPH delivers on its upgraded FY26 guidance (NPAT NZD 450-470M, revenue ~NZD 2.30B) and transitions into FY27 with mid-to-high single-digit revenue growth as hospital volumes normalise post-COVID tailwinds. The stock trades broadly in line with its current A$30.00 price, reflecting a premium quality multiple that is justified but not expanding. Section 122 resolution in July 2026 is neutral-to-mildly positive, and currency remains a modest tailwind. This is the most likely scenario given the guidance upgrade has already been absorbed by the market.
supporting: Guidance upgrade to NZD 2.30B revenue and NZD 450-470M NPAT is officially filed and verified via ASX announcement (22 February 2026)
Forward P/E of 32.9x is elevated but consistent with FPH's historical premium as a high-quality, capital-light medical device compounder
AUD/USD at US$0.70 is supportive for near-term earnings translation without requiring further currency appreciation
FPH's 52-week range of A$29.72-A$34.84 suggests the market has already partially digested the guidance upgrade
contradicting: Trailing P/E of 49.2x implies the market is pricing in sustained high growth that may be difficult to maintain as hospital volumes normalise
Australian Health Department scaling back procurement spending could indirectly pressure hospital budgets and device purchasing cycles
Broker sentiment is described as mixed, with some commentators flagging Section 122 uncertainty as a near-term overhang

hypotheses.title: N3: Risk/Downside
hypotheses.description: Section 122 of the relevant NZ/Australian regulatory framework expires in July 2026 without a favourable resolution, creating reimbursement uncertainty that dampens hospital product demand and forces FPH to revise FY27 guidance downward. Simultaneously, a reversal in AUD/USD toward US$0.75+ erodes the currency tailwind that underpinned the February 2026 guidance upgrade. At 32.9x forward earnings, any guidance miss or growth deceleration triggers a meaningful de-rating toward 25-28x, implying a stock price of A$24-26.
supporting: Section 122 expiry in July 2026 is explicitly flagged by StocksDownUnder as a source of uncertainty that 'will weigh on the share price'
Pinnacle Investment Management ceased to be a substantial holder on 17 March 2026, reducing from 29.65M to 29.22M shares — a potential leading indicator of institutional caution
Australian Health Department scaling back procurement spending signals a tighter hospital budget environment
Currency tailwinds are inherently transient; AUD/USD already moved -0.5% today, and any NZD weakening would reverse the earnings translation benefit
contradicting: FPH's guidance upgrade was issued just one month ago (22 February 2026) and is based on verified ASX filings, reducing near-term downside risk
FPH has a long track record of conservative guidance and consistent delivery, making a near-term miss less likely
Global healthcare spending growth of 35% over five years provides structural demand support that partially offsets cyclical procurement headwinds

hypotheses.title: N4: Disruption/Catalyst
hypotheses.description: A strategic acquirer (e.g., a large-cap US medtech such as Medtronic, BD, or Abbott) makes a takeover approach for FPH, attracted by its proprietary respiratory therapy platform, strong IP moat, and NZD-denominated earnings at a discount to USD peers. Alternatively, a major new clinical indication for Optiflow or a breakthrough in home-use respiratory therapy receives regulatory approval, dramatically expanding FPH's addressable market. Either scenario would drive the stock well above A$40, potentially toward A$50-60.
supporting: FPH's EV/EBITDA of 25.1x and strong IP position in high-flow nasal therapy make it an attractive strategic asset for global medtech consolidators
Global medicines and healthcare spending growth of 35% over five years increases strategic value of premium device platforms
FPH's dual NZX/ASX listing and NZD earnings base could represent a currency arbitrage opportunity for a USD-reporting acquirer
contradicting: No current evidence of M&A interest or strategic review in any ASX announcements or media coverage
FPH's market cap of A$17.6B makes it a very large acquisition target, limiting the pool of potential acquirers
Management has not signalled any strategic alternatives; the company appears focused on organic execution of its hospital growth strategy

narrative.theNarrative: [Coverage Initiated 22-Mar-26 18:22 AEST] Fisher & Paykel Healthcare enters our coverage universe at A$30.00 — a price that sits just 0.9% above its 52-week low of A$29.72 and well below its 52-week high of A$34.84, suggesting the market is in a 'wait and see' mode despite a meaningful guidance upgrade issued just one month ago. On 22 February 2026, FPH upgraded its FY26 guidance to approximately NZD 2.30 billion in operating revenue and NPAT of NZD 450-470 million, citing favourable exchange rates and continued growth in its hospital product portfolio — the Optiflow high-flow nasal therapy franchise that has become a global standard of care in ICU and general ward settings. The guidance upgrade is verified via official ASX filing and represents a material positive, yet the stock has drifted toward its lows in the weeks since, implying the market is discounting a near-term overhang. The primary source of that overhang appears to be Section 122 — a regulatory provision whose expiry in July 2026 introduces uncertainty around reimbursement or product access that commentators at StocksDownUnder have flagged as a weight on the share price. Compounding this, Pinnacle Investment Management ceased to be a substantial holder on 17 March 2026, reducing its stake from 29.65 million to 29.22 million shares — a modest but symbolically notable institutional reduction. On valuation, FPH trades at a forward P/E of 32.9x and EV/EBITDA of 25.1x, multiples that are demanding but historically consistent with FPH's quality premium; the trailing P/E of 49.2x reflects the earnings recovery trajectory and is less relevant on a forward basis. The structural case for FPH remains intact: global healthcare spending grew 35% over the past five years, hospital respiratory therapy adoption continues to expand, and FPH's consumables-driven revenue model provides high earnings visibility. The key near-term test is the FY26 full-year result (expected around May 2026) and the July 2026 Section 122 resolution — together, these two events will determine whether the current A$30.00 price represents a buying opportunity in a quality compounder or a value trap at a premium multiple facing a regulatory inflection.

priceImplication.label: Embedded Assumptions at A$30.00
priceImplication.content:
At A$30.00, the current price appears to embed:

• FY26 NPAT delivery at or near the midpoint of NZD 450-470M guidance — no material upside surprise assumed

• Mid-single-digit FY27 revenue growth (~6-8%) as hospital product volumes normalise post-COVID-era tailwinds, consistent with a 32.9x forward P/E

• Currency neutrality — the AUD/USD tailwind (currently US$0.70) is assumed to be partially transient and not extrapolated into FY27 earnings

• Section 122 uncertainty — the market appears to be applying a modest discount to reflect the unresolved July 2026 regulatory expiry, consistent with the stock's proximity to its 52-week low

• No M&A premium — the current price embeds zero probability of a strategic transaction despite FPH's attractive IP and global platform

• Institutional caution — Pinnacle's reduction below the 5% threshold on 17 March 2026 is consistent with a market that is not aggressively accumulating at current levels

narrative.evidenceCheck: The evidence base for this initiation is moderate in breadth but uneven in depth. The strongest evidence comes from the verified ASX filing of 22 February 2026 (Card 2), which provides a reliable anchor for FY26 financial expectations, and the ownership change filing of 17 March 2026 (Card 10), which is factually precise. Economic data (Card 5) on AUD/USD is current and directly relevant to FPH's earnings translation. Academic/industry data (Card 7) on global healthcare spending growth provides useful structural context. However, significant gaps exist across several domains: there are no specific broker price targets or consensus estimates (Card 3 is qualitative only), no competitor disclosures or peer benchmarking (Card 4), no alternative data on operational metrics (Card 6), no leadership or governance data (Card 9), and the Section 122 regulatory risk (Cards 3 and 8) is mentioned but not quantified or sourced to primary regulatory documents. The evidence is internally consistent — all verified data points to a company executing well in the near term — but the absence of competitive and regulatory primary data means the downside scenario (N3) is harder to calibrate than the base case.
narrative.narrativeStability: The current market narrative — quality compounder with near-term regulatory overhang — is moderately stable but faces two near-term tests that could materially shift it in either direction. The first test is the FY26 full-year result (expected around May 2026): delivery at or above the top of the NZD 450-470M NPAT guidance range would reinforce the bull case and likely push the stock back toward its 52-week high of A$34.84, while any miss would validate the de-rating scenario. The second and more consequential test is the Section 122 expiry in July 2026: a favourable resolution (extension, replacement, or benign expiry) would remove the primary overhang and could catalyse a re-rating, while an adverse outcome could force a reassessment of FPH's hospital product revenue trajectory. The narrative is also vulnerable to currency reversal — if AUD/USD strengthens materially from US$0.70, the FY26 earnings translation tailwind that drove the guidance upgrade would partially unwind, potentially creating a negative surprise in FY27 guidance. Until July 2026, the narrative is likely to remain range-bound, with the stock oscillating between A$29-34 absent a major catalyst.

evidence.intro: Evidence is assessed across ten epistemic domains, ranked by reliability. Each domain carries a distinct epistemic quality — from verified regulatory filings to unverified media commentary — ensuring the analyst understands what the evidence is before assessing what it means.

cards.title: 1. Corporate Communications
cards.finding: Fisher & Paykel Healthcare (FPH) announced on February 22, 2026, an upgrade to its FY26 revenue and earnings guidance. The company now expects full-year operating revenue to be approximately $2.30 billion and net profit after tax (NPAT) to be in the range of $450 million to $470 million, citing favorable exchange rates and continued growth in its hospital product portfolio.
cards.tension: While the upgraded guidance presents a positive outlook, it is a forward-looking statement from the company, which may be inherently optimistic and subject to unforeseen market changes or currency fluctuations.

cards.title: 2. Regulatory Filings & Financial Statements
cards.finding: On February 22, 2026, FPH filed an official ASX announcement detailing its updated FY26 guidance. This filing confirms the expectation of approximately $2.30 billion in operating revenue and a net profit after tax between $450 million and $470 million, an increase from previous guidance.
cards.tension: Although the guidance is officially filed and verified, it represents a forecast rather than actual historical financial performance, and future results could deviate from these projections.

cards.title: 3. Broker Research
cards.finding: News articles suggest that analysts are evaluating FPH positively following its upgraded guidance, with some questioning if it's a 'Buy After Upgraded Guidance'. However, one source notes that 'uncertainty will weigh on the share price' until clarity on Section 122 expires in July, indicating a mixed sentiment among market commentators.
cards.tension: The provided data lacks specific broker reports, consensus estimates, or price targets, making it difficult to quantify the market's modeled expectations or the extent of any potential upside or downside identified by professional analysts.

cards.title: 4. Competitor Disclosures
cards.finding: The provided information does not contain any specific competitor disclosures, announcements, or financial results that would allow for a direct comparison of FPH's performance against its peers in the medical instruments and supplies sector.
cards.tension: Without direct competitor data, it is challenging to assess FPH's market share dynamics, competitive positioning, or how its recent guidance upgrade compares to the broader industry trends or peer performance.

cards.title: 5. Economic Data
cards.finding: FPH explicitly cited 'favourable exchange rates' as a factor contributing to its upgraded FY26 guidance. The current AUD/USD exchange rate is US$0.7, with a -0.5% change today, suggesting that a weaker AUD (or stronger NZD relative to USD, given FPH's NZ base) has provided a tailwind for its reported earnings.
cards.tension: While current exchange rates are favorable, currency markets are inherently volatile. Future adverse movements in the AUD/USD or NZD/USD exchange rates could negatively impact FPH's reported revenue and profit, potentially offsetting operational gains.

cards.title: 6. Alternative Data
cards.finding: The available data does not include specific alternative data points such as real-time operational metrics, ESG performance indicators, customer sentiment analysis, or channel checks for Fisher & Paykel Healthcare.
cards.tension: Without alternative data, it is difficult to gain granular, real-time insights into FPH's operational efficiency, customer satisfaction, or supply chain health, which could provide a more nuanced view beyond official corporate statements.

cards.title: 7. Academic Research
cards.finding: Global spending on medicines grew by 35% over the past five years, according to the IHDS's 'The Global Use of Medicines 2024 - Outlook' report. This indicates a robust and growing global healthcare market, which provides a supportive macro environment for medical device companies like FPH.
cards.tension: While the overall global medicines market is expanding, this trend does not directly guarantee growth in specific medical device sub-sectors or for individual companies. FPH's specific product categories may experience different growth rates or competitive pressures.
cards.source: pharmaphorum.com ('Global medicines and the future outlook')

cards.title: 8. Media & Social
cards.finding: Financial media widely reported FPH's upgraded FY26 guidance, highlighting growth in hospital products and favorable exchange rates. However, some commentary from StocksDownUnder.com suggests that 'uncertainty will weigh on the share price' due to the expiration of Section 122 in July, indicating a potential unquantified risk.
cards.tension: Media coverage can be influenced by short-term news cycles and may not always provide a comprehensive, unbiased view. The mention of 'Section 122' introduces a potential regulatory or policy risk that is not fully explained or verified within the provided data.

cards.title: 9. Leadership & Governance
cards.finding: The provided data does not contain any specific information regarding changes in FPH's leadership, details of insider trading activity, or comprehensive insights into its corporate governance practices.
cards.tension: Without data on leadership stability, insider transactions, or governance quality, it is difficult to assess potential risks or strengths related to the company's management effectiveness, alignment of interests, or ethical oversight.

cards.title: 10. Ownership & Capital Flows
cards.finding: Pinnacle Investment Management Group Limited ceased to be a substantial holder in FPH on March 17, 2026. Their total holding decreased from 29,649,653 shares to 29,219,265 shares, dropping below the 5% substantial holding threshold under New Zealand securities law.
cards.tension: The reduction in holding by a significant institutional investor like Pinnacle could signal a decrease in conviction or a reallocation of capital, potentially exerting selling pressure on the stock. However, the reduction was relatively minor (approx. 1.45% of their prior holding) and may not indicate a fundamental negative view.
cards.source: ASX Announcement 'Ceasing to be a substantial holder' (2026-03-17), filingreader.com

discriminators.intro: The following data points carry high diagnosticity — they can meaningfully shift probability between competing hypotheses.

rows.evidence: Section 122 regulatory expiry outcome (July 2026) — whether the reimbursement/access framework for FPH's hospital products is preserved, improved, or adversely changed
rows.currentReading: Unresolved — expiry due July 2026; market applying a modest discount per StocksDownUnder commentary

rows.evidence: FY26 full-year NPAT delivery vs NZD 450-470M guidance range, and FY27 revenue growth guidance quantum
rows.currentReading: Guidance upgraded 22 February 2026; FY26 result expected May 2026 — currently on track per management

rows.evidence: Institutional ownership trends — whether Pinnacle's reduction below 5% threshold on 17 March 2026 is followed by further institutional selling or is an isolated rebalancing event
rows.currentReading: Pinnacle reduced from 29.65M to 29.22M shares (approx. 1.45% reduction in holding); below 5% NZ substantial holder threshold

discriminators.nonDiscriminating:
• Dividend yield of 1.3% — too small to be a meaningful driver of institutional allocation decisions or to discriminate between growth scenarios; FPH is not a yield stock
  • Total debt of A$148M — negligible relative to A$17.6B market cap and A$697M EBITDA; balance sheet strength is a given for FPH and does not differentiate between hypotheses
  • Global medicines spending growth of 35% over five years — a broad structural tailwind that applies equally to all scenarios and does not help discriminate between FPH-specific bull and bear cases
  • EV/EBITDA of 25.1x — a valuation observation rather than a discriminating data point; the multiple is consistent with FPH's historical range and does not by itself indicate whether the stock is cheap or expensive without a view on EBITDA growth
  • 52-week range of A$29.72-A$34.84 — technical price history provides context but does not discriminate between the fundamental hypotheses driving the stock's future direction

tripwires.intro: The following catalysts could force a material revision to hypothesis weightings. Each carries conditional outcomes that map to specific narrative shifts.

conditions.if: If FY26 NPAT is at or above NZD 470M (top of guidance) and FY27 revenue growth guidance exceeds 10%
conditions.then: Then N1 bull case gains probability; stock likely re-rates toward A$34-40; upgrade to Overweight warranted

conditions.if: If FY26 NPAT is below NZD 450M (misses guidance) or FY27 guidance implies growth below 5%
conditions.then: Then N3 bear case gains probability; stock likely de-rates toward A$25-27; downgrade to Underweight warranted

cards.name: Section 122 Regulatory Expiry

conditions.if: If Section 122 expires or is replaced with a neutral-to-positive reimbursement framework that preserves hospital product access
conditions.then: Then the primary near-term overhang is removed; stock likely recovers toward A$33-36 as institutional buyers re-engage

conditions.if: If Section 122 expiry results in adverse reimbursement changes or product access restrictions for FPH's hospital portfolio
conditions.then: Then N3 bear case is materially strengthened; hospital product revenue trajectory is impaired; stock could fall toward A$24-26

cards.source: StocksDownUnder.com commentary on Section 122; Kalkine.com.au FPH guidance article

cards.name: AUD/USD Currency Check

conditions.if: If AUD/USD remains at or below US$0.70 through end of FY26 (March 2026) and into FY27 guidance period
conditions.then: Then currency tailwind is sustained, supporting FY27 earnings translation and reinforcing the base case at current prices

conditions.if: If AUD/USD strengthens materially above US$0.74-0.75, reversing the tailwind cited in the February 2026 guidance upgrade
conditions.then: Then FY27 earnings translation headwind emerges; market may revise down NZD-to-AUD earnings expectations, pressuring the stock toward A$27-28

cards.source: Macro & Commodity Context (AUD/USD=X); Kalkine.com.au FPH guidance article citing 'favourable exchange rates'

coverageRows.confidence: High — verified ASX/NZX filing with specific NPAT and revenue guidance
High — official ASX filings; ownership change is factually precise
Low — no specific price targets, consensus EPS estimates, or formal broker reports available
Low — no peer benchmarking data; ResMed, Philips Respironics, and Vapotherm not assessed
Medium — AUD/USD current but NZD/USD (more directly relevant to FPH) not separately provided
Low — no channel checks, hospital procurement data, or operational metrics available
Medium — structural macro context is relevant but not FPH-specific; no clinical trial data on Optiflow indications
Medium — multiple sources confirm guidance upgrade narrative; Section 122 risk flagged but not quantified
Low — no data on CEO/CFO tenure, insider transactions, board composition, or remuneration structure
Medium — Pinnacle reduction is precisely documented; broader institutional ownership register not available

couldntAssess: Specific broker consensus EPS and price target distribution
ResMed, Philips Respironics, and Vapotherm competitive positioning and market share data
NZD/USD exchange rate (more directly relevant than AUD/USD for FPH's NZD-reporting base)
Section 122 primary regulatory text and quantified financial impact
Hospital procurement channel checks and Optiflow unit volume data
FPH management insider trading activity and director share transactions
FPH's R&D pipeline beyond current Optiflow platform
FPH's geographic revenue breakdown (US vs Europe vs Asia-Pacific)
Short interest and borrow cost data for FPH on ASX
gaps.analyticalLimitations: This initiation is constrained by the absence of formal broker research, competitive benchmarking, and primary regulatory data on Section 122. The Section 122 risk — the most important near-term discriminator between the bull and bear cases — cannot be quantified from available data, which limits the precision of the N3 downside scenario. The analysis relies heavily on FPH's own guidance (a motivated source) and media commentary (an unverified source) for the forward earnings outlook. Without NZD/USD spot data, the currency tailwind analysis is approximated using AUD/USD. The valuation analysis is based on reported multiples rather than a bottom-up DCF or sum-of-the-parts, which would require segment-level revenue and margin data not currently available. These limitations are inherent to an initiation based on publicly available data and would be addressed in a full coverage build with primary research.

trend.structure: Price in downtrend relative to 50-day and 200-day moving averages.

footer.disclaimer: This report does not constitute personal financial advice. Continuum Intelligence synthesises cross-domain evidence using the Analysis of Competing Hypotheses (ACH) methodology. All factual claims are sourced from ASX filings, company disclosures, broker consensus data, and publicly available information.
